India.name: India
India.dietary_notes: World's highest vegetarian population (~30-40%). Strong Ayurvedic food traditions.
cultural_restrictions: Jain: strict vegetarian, no root vegetables

Kerala.name: Kerala
Kerala.dietary_notes: Highest fish consumption in India. Beef consumed by Christians & Muslims.

Tamil Nadu.name: Tamil Nadu
Tamil Nadu.dietary_notes: Strong fermented food tradition (idli/dosa batter). Ragi is calcium-rich.

Karnataka.name: Karnataka
Karnataka.dietary_notes: Ragi culture — exceptional calcium (344mg/100g). Millet diversity.

Andhra Pradesh.name: Andhra Pradesh
Andhra Pradesh.dietary_notes: Spiciest cuisine in India. Very high chili intake (capsaicin benefits).

Telangana.name: Telangana
Telangana.dietary_notes: Hyderabadi Mughal cuisine influence — rich preparations.

Maharashtra.name: Maharashtra
Maharashtra.dietary_notes: Jowar bhakri is gluten-free, high-fiber staple. Kokum has anti-obesity properties.

Gujarat.name: Gujarat
Gujarat.dietary_notes: ~60% vegetarian. Strong fermented food tradition (dhokla, handvo). Sweet-sour-spicy profile.

Rajasthan.name: Rajasthan
Rajasthan.dietary_notes: Arid climate — dried food preservation. High dairy/ghee intake. Bajra is iron-rich.

Punjab.name: Punjab
traditional_dishes: sarson ka saag + makki ki roti
Punjab.dietary_notes: Highest dairy consumption region. Tandoori cooking is healthy. Large portions.

West Bengal.name: West Bengal
West Bengal.dietary_notes: Fish is identity food. Mustard oil is primary fat (rich in omega-3 ALA).

Uttar Pradesh.name: Uttar Pradesh
Uttar Pradesh.dietary_notes: Sattu (roasted chickpea flour) is an excellent protein supplement.

Odisha.name: Odisha
Odisha.dietary_notes: Pakhala (fermented rice) is an excellent probiotic food.

Himachal Pradesh.name: Himachal Pradesh
Himachal Pradesh.dietary_notes: Apple region — good antioxidant source. River trout for omega-3.

Assam.name: Assam
Assam.dietary_notes: Strong fish culture. Bhut jolokia (ghost pepper) has extreme capsaicin.

Sikkim.name: Sikkim
Sikkim.dietary_notes: Tibetan/Nepali influence. Rich fermented food tradition.

Pakistan.name: Pakistan
Pakistan.dietary_notes: Halal dietary requirements. Meat consumption higher than India.
cultural_restrictions: Muslim: halal, no pork, Ramadan fasting

Bangladesh.name: Bangladesh
Bangladesh.dietary_notes: Fish is central to diet. Mustard oil is primary cooking fat.

Sri Lanka.name: Sri Lanka
Sri Lanka.dietary_notes: Ceylon cinnamon is premium quality — anti-inflammatory, blood sugar regulating.

Nepal.name: Nepal
Nepal.dietary_notes: Dal-bhat-tarkari is daily staple. Strong fermented food tradition (gundruk, sinki).

China.name: China
China.dietary_notes: Eight Great Cuisines (八大菜系). High soy intake. Green tea tradition.

Sichuan.name: Sichuan
Sichuan.dietary_notes: Sichuan peppercorn has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.

Guangdong.name: Guangdong
Guangdong.dietary_notes: Healthiest Chinese cuisine — emphasis on steaming, fresh ingredients. Medicinal soups.

Yunnan.name: Yunnan
Yunnan.dietary_notes: World's most diverse mushroom consumption. Pu-erh tea for gut health.

Shandong.name: Shandong
Shandong.dietary_notes: Garlic capital of China. Sea cucumber for collagen/joint health.

Fujian.name: Fujian
Fujian.dietary_notes: Red yeast rice contains natural statin (monacolin K) — cardiovascular benefits.

Hunan.name: Hunan
Hunan.dietary_notes: Fresh chili provides vitamin C + capsaicin. Smoked meat tradition.

Japan.name: Japan
Japan.dietary_notes: Washoku (traditional Japanese cuisine) is UNESCO heritage. Hara hachi bu (eat 80% full).

Okinawa.name: Okinawa
staple_foods: sweet potato (purple and orange — primary, not rice)
Okinawa.dietary_notes: BLUE ZONE — world's highest longevity. Caloric restriction + nutrient density. Purple sweet potato (anthocyanins).

Hokkaido.name: Hokkaido
Hokkaido.dietary_notes: Japan's dairy region. Wild Pacific salmon (astaxanthin + omega-3).

Tokyo.name: Tokyo
Tokyo.dietary_notes: Natto (vitamin K2, nattokinase — cardiovascular/bone health). Soba (rutin).

Kyoto.name: Kyoto
Kyoto.dietary_notes: Matcha (10x antioxidants of green tea). Shojin ryori is entirely plant-based Buddhist cuisine.

South Korea.name: South Korea
South Korea.dietary_notes: Kimchi in every meal — excellent probiotic. Fermented food culture (doenjang, gochujang).

Thailand.name: Thailand
Thailand.dietary_notes: Fresh herbs dominant (anti-inflammatory, antioxidant). Fish sauce is universal condiment.

Vietnam.name: Vietnam
Vietnam.dietary_notes: Emphasis on fresh herbs and raw vegetables. Lighter than other Southeast Asian cuisines.

Indonesia.name: Indonesia
Indonesia.dietary_notes: Tempeh is Indonesian invention — excellent probiotic/protein. World's largest Muslim population.

Turkey.name: Turkey
Turkey.dietary_notes: Bridge between Mediterranean and Middle Eastern cuisines. Strong yogurt/fermented dairy culture.
cultural_restrictions: Muslim: halal, no pork, Ramadan fasting

Iran.name: Iran
Iran.dietary_notes: Saffron is most expensive spice — anti-depressant, antioxidant properties. Strong nut culture.
cultural_restrictions: Muslim: halal, no pork, no alcohol, Ramadan fasting

Saudi Arabia.name: Saudi Arabia
Saudi Arabia.dietary_notes: Dates are central — excellent minerals, fiber. Arabic coffee with cardamom tradition.
cultural_restrictions: Muslim: halal, no pork, no alcohol, Ramadan fasting

United Kingdom.name: United Kingdom
United Kingdom.dietary_notes: Growing plant-based movement. Multicultural cuisine (Indian food is 'national dish').

France.name: France
France.dietary_notes: French Paradox — high saturated fat with low CVD (wine, portion control, meal structure).

Germany.name: Germany
Germany.dietary_notes: Strong fermented food tradition (sauerkraut — probiotics). Rye bread is high fiber.

Italy.name: Italy
Italy.dietary_notes: Mediterranean diet origin. Southern Italy & Sardinia (Blue Zone) have lowest CVD in Italy.

Sicily.name: Sicily
Sicily.dietary_notes: Arab, Greek, Norman influences. Pistachio capital.

Tuscany.name: Tuscany
Tuscany.dietary_notes: Simple, ingredient-focused cooking. Excellent olive oil. Farro (ancient grain).

Spain.name: Spain
Spain.dietary_notes: Mediterranean diet. Olive oil is primary fat. Late meal times (lunch 2-3pm, dinner 9-10pm).

Greece.name: Greece
Greece.dietary_notes: Classic Mediterranean diet. Wild greens (horta) tradition — exceptional antioxidants.

Nigeria.name: Nigeria
Nigeria.dietary_notes: Highly diverse by region and ethnicity. Palm oil provides tocotrienols. Locust beans are probiotic.

Lagos.name: Lagos
Lagos.dietary_notes: Urban diet — diverse. Yoruba food culture with diverse leafy soups.

Kano.name: Kano
Kano.dietary_notes: Hausa-Fulani cuisine. Baobab has 6x vitamin C of oranges. Millet is iron/zinc-rich.

Enugu.name: Enugu
Enugu.dietary_notes: Bitter leaf (Vernonia amygdalina) has anti-diabetic, hepatoprotective properties.

Ethiopia.name: Ethiopia
Ethiopia.dietary_notes: Teff is exceptionally high in iron and calcium. Ethiopian Orthodox: ~180 vegan fasting days/year.
cultural_restrictions: Ethiopian Orthodox: extensive vegan fasting (~180 days/year)

Kenya.name: Kenya
Kenya.dietary_notes: Ugali-sukuma wiki is daily staple. Sukuma wiki (kale) provides iron, calcium, vitamin K.

South Africa.name: South Africa
South Africa.dietary_notes: Rainbow nation — very diverse cuisine. Cape Malay, Dutch, Indian, Indigenous influences.

United States.name: United States
United States.dietary_notes: Standard American Diet (SAD) is reference for unhealthy patterns. BUT great diversity available.

California.name: California
California.dietary_notes: Highest vegetable/fruit consumption in US. Health-conscious food culture.

Louisiana.name: Louisiana
Louisiana.dietary_notes: Creole/Cajun cuisine — French, African, Spanish influences. Crawfish is lean protein.

Texas.name: Texas
Texas.dietary_notes: Tex-Mex and BBQ culture. Beans are daily. High red meat consumption.

Hawaii.name: Hawaii
Hawaii.dietary_notes: Pacific Islander + Asian + American fusion. Taro is resistant starch.

New York.name: New York
proteins: diverse — NYC is world's most diverse food city
New York.dietary_notes: Most diverse food city on earth — access to every cuisine.

Mexico.name: Mexico
Mexico.dietary_notes: Corn+beans = complete protein. Nixtamalization releases niacin, increases calcium.

Oaxaca.name: Oaxaca
Oaxaca.dietary_notes: Land of Seven Moles. Chapulines (72% protein, sustainable). Heritage corn varieties.

Yucatan.name: Yucatan
Yucatan.dietary_notes: Chaya is higher in protein, calcium, iron than spinach. Maya heritage cuisine.

Brazil.name: Brazil
Brazil.dietary_notes: Daily rice-and-beans is complementary protein, high fiber. Açaí is powerful antioxidant. Brazilian dietary guidelines are internationally praised.

Amazonas.name: Amazonas
Amazonas.dietary_notes: Brazil nuts — 1 nut = daily selenium. Açaí — highest ORAC antioxidant value.

Bahia.name: Bahia
fruits: cashew fruit (5x vitamin C of orange)
Bahia.dietary_notes: Afro-Brazilian cuisine. Dendê (red palm oil) is richest source of tocotrienols.

Rio Grande do Sul.name: Rio Grande do Sul
Rio Grande do Sul.dietary_notes: Yerba mate (polyphenols, saponins — anti-obesity, lipid-lowering). Extremely high red meat.

Argentina.name: Argentina
Argentina.dietary_notes: World's highest per-capita beef consumption. Mate (yerba mate) is daily ritual.

Peru.name: Peru
Peru.dietary_notes: Quinoa — complete protein, all essential amino acids. Thousands of potato varieties. Camu camu — highest vitamin C.

Australia.name: Australia
Australia.dietary_notes: Aboriginal bush foods are exceptionally nutrient-dense. Kakadu plum = highest vitamin C of any fruit.

New Zealand.name: New Zealand
New Zealand.dietary_notes: Green-lipped mussels contain unique omega-3 (ETA — anti-inflammatory). Kiwifruit is vitamin C powerhouse.
